english: sgo-tém (sgo tem) threshold, also the head-piece of a door. — sgo-bdág (sgo bdag) = sgo-dpón (sgo dpon). — sgo-rnám (sgo rnam) a single board, e.g. of the floor. — sgó-pa (sgo pa), resp. čábs-sgo-pa (chabs sgo pa) door-keeper, porter; sgo-dpón (sgo dpon) the first, principal door-keeper. — *go-pïn* (*go pin*) W. door-hinge. — sgo-pár (sgo par) board or plank of a door Cs. — sgo-bár (sgo bar) Ld. chinks between the separate laths of a door (for of such the doors in Tibet frequently consist, owing to the scarcity of wood). — sgó-ma (sgo ma) 1. pane or square of a door, fold of a folding-door; 2. a masked dancer in religious dramatic entertainments, representing one of the four guardians of the world (v. rgyal-čén (rgyal chen)). — sgó-mo (sgo mo) 1. a large door, a gate, castle-gate, town-gate; 2. beginning, rtsís-kyi sgó-mo (rtsis kyi sgo mo) Pth. = rtsis-go (rtsis go) Cs. (Chronol. Table) beginning of a new epoch. — sgo-mtsáms (sgo mtshams) the small opening left between door-post and door, when the latter does not perfectly fit. — sgo-yíg (sgo yig) Cs. 1. inscription, 2. lampoon, libel, 3. a magisterial advertisement fastened to a door. — sgo-lá (sgo la) n. of a high and difficult mountain-pass between Lhasa and Paṅ (Pang), v. Huc. I. p. 244. — sgo-sruṅ (sgo srung) door-keeper, porter Dzl.
in skyé-sgo (skye sgo) v. sub sgó-po (sgo po).

or sgoṅ-ṅá (sgong nga) and sgoṅ (sgong), egg, eggs, spawn, also egg as a measure Lt.; sgoṅ-dkrís (sgong dkris) the pellicle, membrane of an egg Sch.; sgóṅ-čú (sgong chu) the white of an egg Sch.; sgoṅ-šún (sgong shun), or kog (kog), the shell of an egg; sgoṅ-sér (sgong ser) yolk of an egg Sch. — sgo-ṅa ṗyed (sgo nga phyed) a scholastic term, v. Was. (274).

also sgó-bo (sgo bo), (Ld. *gó-po* (*go po*)) W. 1. the body, with respect to its physical nature and appearance, *gó-po čén-mo* (*go po chen mo*), riṅ-mo (ring mo), go-riṅ (go ring), go-záṅ* (*go zang*) tall, *gó-po čúṅ-se* (*go po chung se*) of small stature, short; *róm-po* (*rom po*) stout, lusty; *t'á-mo* (*tha mo*) slender, thin; *dé-mo* (*de mo*) healthy, well; *go-yál* (*go yal*) a man that has lost his own body by gaming and become the slave of another. — 2. = skyé-sgo (skye sgo) face, countenance, skye-sgo legs (skye sgo legs) a beautiful face, ṭuṅ-sgo (ṭung sgo) an ugly face Mil. — sgó-lo (sgo lo) 1. body, 2. face, as a flattering word; also directly for a nice or pretty face, *gó-lo min dug bag tsogs yod* (*go lo min dug bag tshogs yod*) she has not a pretty face, but looks like a fright W.

pf. bsgo (bsgo) (bsgos (bsgos) in Lexx., prob. obsolete) to say, when used of superiors, hence mostly to bid, to order (cf. the article bka (bka) init.), frq. in early literature, in later times more and more disappearing, being unknown to the common people.
1. v. sgó-po (sgo po). — 2. Ld. also = sgo-ṅá (sgo nga).

(Ssk. लशुन) garlic, leek, (Allium); ri-sgóg (ri sgog) Med. Allium sphaeroceph. L., or a species allied to it; sgog-skyá (sgog skya) Med. Allium nivale Jacqm. (?); sgog-sṅón (sgog sngon) Med. perh. A. rubellum, a blue species, very common in the Himalaya. — sgog-tíṅ (sgog ting) mortar, sgog-gtúṅ (sgog gtung) pestle, for bruising leek.

1. Cs.: 'pf. bsgags (bsgags), fut. bsgag (bsgag), to make one swear, sgóg-po (sgog po) one that makes a person swear.' I only met with mna-sgóg (mna sgog) Lex. w. e. — 2. rya sgóg-pa (rya sgog pa) v. rya (rya).
1. v. sgo-ṅá (sgo nga). — 2. n. of a country, prob. = koṅ (kong) Glr. — 3. sgoṅ-tóg-pa (sgong tog pa) n. of a plant Med.

pf. bsgoṅs (bsgongs), fut. bsgoṅ (bsgong), imp. sgoṅ (sgong) (s), 1. to make round, globular Cs.; so it is prob. to be understood in: bu-rám bsgár-žiṅ bsgráṅs-nas bsgoṅs (bu ram bsgar zhing bsgrangs nas bsgongs) Lex., he having boiled down the sugar and allowed it to grow cold, formed it into balls (in this form the sugar is usually kept in Tibet). — 2. to hide or conceal a thing Sch., thus in *góṅ-te bór-če* (*gong te bor che*) W.; cf also dpá-sgoṅ-ba (dpa sgong ba).
unable, deficient, wanting in strength Sch.; *lág-pa gob-soṅ* (*lag pa gob song*) Kun. the hands are unable (to move), stiff (from cold).
reflection, meditation, contemplation, sgom šór-gyi dógs-pa (sgom shor gyi dogs pa) the fear lest contemplation should be prejudiced
